About Us

A Robotics Club Born
from Passion

Moon Robotics (MoonBots VEX 862) is an Auckland-based VEX Robotics Club founded in 2024 by a father-son duo who have lived the VEX journey from the sidelines all the way to the world stage.

We serve students across Central Auckland, North Shore, East & West Auckland, offering free workshops, structured training programmes, and competitive team membership.

Our mission: make STEM education fun, accessible, and inspiring for every young learner aged 6–14, regardless of prior experience.

Our Story

From Parent Volunteer
to Club Founder

2000

The Journey Begins

Evan joins Lynfield College’s robotics club. Jason becomes a dedicated robotics parent — driving to competitions, cheering from the sidelines, watching his son discover his passion.

2020–2023

Champion Years

Evan competes at VEX Worlds multiple times, claiming National Champion and World Champion titles. Jason rises through the volunteer corps to become Auckland Head Referee.

🏆 VEX IQ World Championship 2020-21 🏆 VEX V5 National Champion 2022 🏆 VEX V5 World Champion 2022
2024

Moon Robotics Founded

Father and son launch Moon Robotics (VEX 862), channeling years of competition experience into building the next generation of young engineers across Auckland.

2024–2026

Dual Volunteer of the Year

Both Jason and Evan receive VEX New Zealand Volunteer of the Year awards in consecutive seasons — a remarkable father-son achievement.

🏉 VEX NZ Volunteer of the Year 2024-25 — Evan
🏉 VEX NZ Volunteer of the Year 2025-26 — Jason

Programs

How to Join

Three pathways from zero experience to competition-ready.

🎓

Free Workshop

Drop-in sessions at schools, libraries, and community events across Auckland. No registration — just show up and try robotics for free.

FREE
🔧

10-Session Training

Structured beginner programme covering VEX IQ building, mechanical design, and programming. Perfect for students aged 6–14.

Enrol Now
🏆

Competition Team

Join a VEX team for the annual season. Design, build, and compete at Auckland regional events and the NZ National Championship.

Apply

VEX Robotics

About VEX Robotics

VEX Robotics is the world’s largest and fastest-growing robotics competition, running across 70+ countries and organised by the Robotics Education & Competition Foundation (RECF).

Moon Robotics offers two VEX programmes in Auckland:

VEX IQ — Ages 8–14. Snap-together plastic components. Focuses on teamwork, design, and driver skills. Perfect for beginners.
VEX V5 — Ages 12+. Metal construction, V5 smart motors, and advanced programming. For students ready to go deeper.
Season runs September through April, culminating in the VEX Robotics World Championship.
Teams are evaluated on performance, engineering documentation, and interview presentation.
70+

KIWIbots NZ

VEX Robotics in New Zealand

KIWIbots is New Zealand’s official organiser for VEX IQ and V5 Robotics competitions, running regional qualifiers and the National Championship each year.

Moon Robotics is proud to be an active part of the KIWIbots community. Both our mentors serve as Auckland Head Referees and have been recognised as VEX NZ Volunteers of the Year.

→
Annual VEX IQ and V5 competitions held across New Zealand
→
Regional qualifiers in Auckland, Wellington, Christchurch, and more
→
National Champions qualify to represent NZ at the VEX World Championship
→
Skills-only events and scrimmages throughout the season
